5. **Timestamps**: Notable moments with their timestamps (in the summary file)

Save the full transcript to `transcript.txt` and the structured summary to `video_summary.md`.

{"type": "toolCall", "name": "write_file", "arguments": {"content": "# Video Summary: Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster)

## Metadata

| Field       | Value                                                       |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Title**   | Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster) |
| **Channel** | Rick Astley                                                 |
| **Duration**| 3 minutes 33 seconds                                        |
| **Upload Date** | October 24, 2009                                        |
| **URL**     | https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ                 |

---

## Summary (200–300 words)

"Never Gonna Give You Up" is a classic pop song by British singer Rick Astley, released in 1987 and written by the production trio Stock Aitken Waterman. This official music video, uploaded to Rick Astley's verified YouTube channel in October 2009, has been remastered in 4K and remains one of the most iconic and culturally significant music videos in internet history. The video opens with Rick Astley in a dimly lit, moody setting before transitioning to an energetic choreographed performance featuring backup dancers and a confident, soulful vocal delivery. The song's upbeat synth-pop sound is driven by an infectious drum machine, funky bassline, and bright keyboard melodies.

Lyrically, the song is a straightforward love anthem built on unwavering devotion and loyalty. The narrator promises complete faithfulness to his partner, declaring he will "never give you up, never let you down, never run around and desert you, never make you cry, never say goodbye, and never tell a lie and hurt you." The verses describe a deep, long-standing connection between two people who understand each other's feelings, with the narrator urging honest communication about their mutual affection. The structure alternates between two distinct verses and the unforgettable, repeated chorus that forms the emotional core of the song.

Beyond its musical appeal, this video became the centerpiece of the internet phenomenon known as "Rickrolling" — a bait-and-switch meme in which users are tricked into clicking links that unexpectedly lead to this music video. This has cemented the video's status as a cornerstone of internet culture, giving it renewed relevance across generations.

---

## Key Points

- **Love & Devotion Theme**: The song's central message is one of unconditional commitment and loyalty in a romantic relationship.
- **Iconic Chorus**: The "Never gonna give you up" refrain is one of the most recognizable hooks in pop music history.
- **Stock Aitken Waterman Production**: Written and produced by the legendary British production team responsible for many 1980s pop hits.
- **Rick Astley's Debut Hit**: This was Astley's breakthrough single, launching his career at age 21.
- **Internet Culture Phenomenon**: The video became the foundation of "Rickrolling," one of the most enduring internet memes.
- **4K Remaster**: This restored version provides enhanced visual quality of the original 1980s music video.

---

## Timestamps — Notable Moments

| Time        | Moment                                      |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| 0:00–0:17   | Intro instrumental — moody, atmospheric opening |
| 0:18–0:34   | **Verse 1** — "We're no strangers to love"    |
| 0:35–0:42   | **Pre-Chorus** — "I just wanna tell you how I'm feeling" |
| 0:43–0:58   | **Chorus (1st time)** — "Never gonna give you up" |
| 0:59–1:16   | **Verse 2** — "We've known each other for so long" |
| 1:17–1:24   | **Pre-Chorus (2nd time)**                    |
| 1:25–1:58   | **Chorus (2nd & 3rd times)** — Double repetition with dance sequences |
| 1:59–2:15   | **Bridge / Vocal Ad-libs** — "(Ooh, give you up)" |
| 2:16–2:32   | **Verse 3** — Repeat of Verse 2              |
| 2:33–2:40   | **Pre-Chorus (3rd time)**                    |
| 2:41–3:33   | **Final Choruses** — Extended chorus repetitions leading to fade-out |
", "file_path": "/app/working/workspaces/default/video_summary.md"}}

# Video Summary: Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster)

## Metadata

| Field       | Value                                                       |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Title**   | Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster) |
| **Channel** | Rick Astley                                                 |
| **Duration**| 3 minutes 33 seconds                                        |
| **Upload Date** | October 24, 2009                                        |
| **URL**     | https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ                 |

---

## Summary (200–300 words)

"Never Gonna Give You Up" is a classic pop song by British singer Rick Astley, released in 1987 and written by the production trio Stock Aitken Waterman. This official music video, uploaded to Rick Astley's verified YouTube channel in October 2009, has been remastered in 4K and remains one of the most iconic and culturally significant music videos in internet history. The video opens with Rick Astley in a dimly lit, moody setting before transitioning to an energetic choreographed performance featuring backup dancers and a confident, soulful vocal delivery. The song's upbeat synth-pop sound is driven by an infectious drum machine, funky bassline, and bright keyboard melodies.

Lyrically, the song is a straightforward love anthem built on unwavering devotion and loyalty. The narrator promises complete faithfulness to his partner, declaring he will "never give you up, never let you down, never run around and desert you, never make you cry, never say goodbye, and never tell a lie and hurt you." The verses describe a deep, long-standing connection between two people who understand each other's feelings, with the narrator urging honest communication about their mutual affection. The structure alternates between two distinct verses and the unforgettable, repeated chorus that forms the emotional core of the song.

Beyond its musical appeal, this video became the centerpiece of the internet phenomenon known as "Rickrolling" — a bait-and-switch meme in which users are tricked into clicking links that unexpectedly lead to this music video. This has cemented the video's status as a cornerstone of internet culture, giving it renewed relevance across generations.

---

## Key Points

- **Love & Devotion Theme**: The song's central message is one of unconditional commitment and loyalty in a romantic relationship.
- **Iconic Chorus**: The "Never gonna give you up" refrain is one of the most recognizable hooks in pop music history.
- **Stock Aitken Waterman Production**: Written and produced by the legendary British production team responsible for many 1980s pop hits.
- **Rick Astley's Debut Hit**: This was Astley's breakthrough single, launching his career at age 21.
- **Internet Culture Phenomenon**: The video became the foundation of "Rickrolling," one of the most enduring internet memes.
- **4K Remaster**: This restored version provides enhanced visual quality of the original 1980s music video.

---

## Timestamps — Notable Moments

| 0:43–0:58   | **Chorus (1st time)** — "Never gonna give you up" |
| 0:59–1:16   | **Verse 2** — "We've known each other for so long" |
| 1:17–1:24   | **Pre-Chorus (2nd time)**                    |
| 1:25–1:58   | **Chorus (2nd & 3rd times)** — Double repetition with dance sequences |

| 2:33–2:40   | **Pre-Chorus (3rd time)**                    |
| 2:41–3:33   | **Final Choruses** — Extended chorus repetitions leading to fade-out |
